mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate below 4.5% signals tightening labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in triggered periods show negative or flat performance (mean < 0.1%) with 95% confidence interval overlapping zero.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent increase in residential construction permits (HOUST) and consumer spending (RSAFS) above their 3-month moving averages signals expanding demand conditions that drive S&P 500 positive returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: Interaction term between HOUST and RSAFS shows negative coefficient in 60-day regression models (p > 0.1) or combined momentum fails to outperform baseline SPX returns by 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 12-month trend indicates supply-side disruption that increases equity risk premiums, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in triggered periods exceed 1.5% with 90% confidence, or INDPRO contraction fails to correlate with SPX returns (Pearson r > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI inflation reading above 3.0% triggers central bank policy rate hikes that increase discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in high-inflation periods show positive performance (mean > 0.2%) with 95% confidence or CPIAUCSL fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll gain (PAYEMS) exceeding 200k combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals strong labor market dynamics that drive S&P 500 positive returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Interaction term between PAYEMS and UNRATE shows negative coefficient in 20-day regression models (p > 0.1) or combined signal fails to outperform baseline SPX returns by 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction (GDPC1) below 1.0% signals recession risk that increases equity market volatility, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in triggered periods exceed 3.0% with 90% confidence or GDPC1 contraction fails to correlate with SPX returns (Pearson r > 0.2).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above 104.0 combined with declining capacity utilization signals overheating supply conditions that drive S&P 500 negative returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in triggered periods show positive performance (mean > 0.3%) with 95% confidence or INDPRO surge fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.07).
